author: PC Gamer
text:
How to unlock tools in Outbound: Tips for the Axe, Pickaxe, and other downloads
If Outbound's signal towers have you confused, I've got a few tips for choosing your next download.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Everyone knows Wolfenstein 3D made the FPS a phenomenon. What this mod presupposes is… what if it didn't?
Isowulf does a remarkably faithful job transforming Wolfenstein 3D into an isometric shooter.

author: PC Gamer
text:
16 years after attending a Fallout: New Vegas launch event, Aaron Paul gets cast in Fallout Season 3
He played the long game.

author: PC Gamer
text:
World of Warcraft 'Classic Plus', a version of the old MMO with modern updates, might actually be real as Blizzard hosts secret event with popular streamers
WoW Classic streamers Xaryu, Sodapoppin, and Esfand are among the few invited to Irvine under NDA.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam's most-wishlisted game is out this week following a year of corporate drama that involved one of the most embarrassing ChatGPT uses on record
The moment of truth nears for ultra-anticipated survival game Subnautica 2.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Microsoft fires head of Israeli subsidiary and other managers over surveillance of Palestinians
The move follows years of internal and external pressure.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Game industry lobby group that argued against preservation efforts from libraries is now pushing back on Stop Killing Games, saying it could prevent 'new games, features, and technology'
Your occasional reminder that the Entertainment Software Association is not your friend.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Path of Exile 2's 1.0 release won't need to accommodate other game releases, director says—except GTA 6: 'That's a Goliath I don't want to take on quite frankly'
Even without a PC release, GTA 6 has Grinding Gear Games carefully planning ahead.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Google AI bot put in charge of Swedish coffee shop, proceeds to order 3,000 rubber gloves, 6,000 napkins, 4 first-aid kits, and constantly screws up the bread order
Perhaps there is a subtle message here.

author: PC Gamer
text:
SATA drives are still going strong as Kingston announces it has shipped 100 million A400 SSDs
Perfect for when you're feeling precious about PCIe lanes...

author: PC Gamer
text:
Nine Sols, one of our best-reviewed soulslikes, is just $12 on Steam this week
Not a bad price for near-perfection.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to get the Sigil of Valor in Crimson Desert
Get your pet pooch to bite the enemy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hackers are using fake job interviews to load applicants' PCs with a password-stealing Trojan
No job and a new virus. What a nightmare.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AMD reportedly planning a new budget graphics card with a surprising number of cores
Though much lower clocks.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I've spent 30 years with Microsoft but Linux and Pop! OS might have converted me
Linux has saved me from the grubby claws of Windows hell, but it comes with a catch.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mixtape is at the center of another tedious culture war discourse, and I think I know why
Nostalgia's a powerful thing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to find the correct chemical agents for Anders in Directive 8020
Bypass the puzzle of finding the correct chemical agents in Episode 5.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Lip-Bu Tan says 'Intel and Nvidia are collaborating to develop exciting new products' and I'm wondering what they could possibly be
Presumably involves a lot of AI, but I hope not.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find the key code to Carter's PC in Directive 8020
Uncover the secret to Carter's PC in Episode 2.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Sinking City 2 shifts the series to survival horror, and manages to be genuinely unsettling
The first game was atmospheric, but not really scary. Here's hoping the sequel gets it right.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Today I learned that Gears of War creator Cliff Bleszinski produced a Tony Award-winning musical
CliffyB contains multitudes.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Intel's Big Battlemage Arc Pro B70 GPU benchmarked in games, ends up bang-on Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti 16 GB performance
So much silicon for such modest performance.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We want to start today by apologising': Phasmophobia is fixing issues with its Player Character update after a slew of complaints
The latest patch didn't go down too well.

author: PC Gamer
text:
eBay shoots down 'neither credible nor attractive' $56 billion GameStop offer from CEO who says 'I'm not going away. I'm a pain in the ass'
You didn't win this auction.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Noctua's iconic beige and brown PC fan now comes in a slightly less iconic all-black version for PC builders who can't stand the original colour
It's a bit more versatile but a bit less interesting to me.

author: PC Gamer
text:
MSI MPG 322UR X24 review
The art of QD-OLED very nearly perfected.

text:

Though much lower clocks.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Singleplayer simulated MMO Erenshor is getting raids this summer
You'll be able to join 14 computer-controlled players in an endgame loot grind.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to find the Serial Number for the Probe in Directive 8020
Get straight to sending your signal.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sony assures that AI is only meant to 'augment' artists' capabilities instead of replacing them but it feels like a slippery slope
And that's not including upscaling and PSSR.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New testing shows DisplayPort 2.1 is very picky about the monitor cable you're using
If you want the full 80 Gbps of bandwidth, you'll need a DP80-certified cable.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We're doing something a bit more interesting': All Will Rise dev says its progressive deck-builder shouldn't be dumped in the 'woke, liberal bucket'
A river god has been murdered, and it's up to you to hold the rich and powerful to account.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Popular PlayStation emulator clamps down on AI submissions: 'Leave behind something useful to humanity when you're gone, instead of peddling slop'
'As for all the AI bros seething on our socials, we're simply blocking you.'

author: PC Gamer
text:
Wordle is becoming a primetime TV quiz show, because the ultimate five-letter word is 'money'
The show will be presented by Savannah Guthrie and co-produced by Jimmy Fallon.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We're starting to get convincing counterfeit DDR5 modules, just in case the memory crisis isn't bad enough already
A different kind of memory crisis.

text:
Protesters 'raid' EA to oppose Saudi Arabia acquisition

author: PC Gamer
text:
This vampire farm sim has Real Housewives of Stardew energy
Moonlight Peaks is brave enough to ask: What if Stardew Valley were more toxic?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Former NetEase exec's new investment group is funding Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic because 'everyone wins' if you 'give the best creator the opportunity to work on a dream project'
After raising $100 million, GreaterThan Group is also funding a shooter from former Black Ops design director David Vonderhaar.
